title: AI Trust & Safety Digest
tagline: A curated current snapshot of AI trust, safety, and governance, including as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
lead: This cycle’s strongest signal is a shift from broad safety principles toward more operational mechanisms: secure evaluation infrastructure, external red teaming of monitoring systems, and bug-bounty style discovery of model failures. Labs and standards actors are also making model behavior and manipulation safeguards more explicit, suggesting continued movement toward auditable, testable safety practices rather than purely aspirational commitments.

items.title: CAISI signs CRADA with OpenMined for secure AI evaluations
items.why_it_matters: Secure computation could enable more sensitive model evaluations without exposing models, data, or methods.

items.title: METR red-teams Anthropic’s internal agent monitoring systems
items.why_it_matters: External testing of monitoring defenses offers concrete evidence about agent oversight and security gaps.

items.title: OpenAI launches Safety Bug Bounty program
items.why_it_matters: Bug-bounty incentives can surface abuse pathways, prompt injection, and exfiltration risks earlier.

items.title: Google DeepMind details work on harmful manipulation safeguards
items.why_it_matters: Manipulation research targets subtle model harms that standard toxicity filters may miss.

items.title: OpenAI explains its approach to the Model Spec
items.why_it_matters: A public behavior specification makes model conduct more legible for auditing and accountability.

items.title: Partnership on AI examines assurance for trustworthy AI systems
items.why_it_matters: Assurance frameworks could shape how safety claims are evidenced, reviewed, and governed.

items.title: OpenAI releases teen safety policies for developers
items.why_it_matters: Age-specific safeguards help developers manage deployment risks for minors more systematically.

note: Selected from recent trust-and-safety relevant posts and papers by labs, evaluators, standards bodies, policy organizations, and arXiv, then summarized with GPT-5.4. Includes as many materially important fresh items as the day warrants.
